title: Today's NYT Mini Crossword Answers for Saturday, March 7
description: Here are the answers for The New York Times Mini Crossword for March 7.

title: Kids Online Safety Act Advances to House Amid Concerns Over Free Speech and Civil Rights
description: Critics of the act say measures like age verification could have harmful effects.

title: From Teacher to Fashion Brand Founder
description:
In 2019 Nasrin Jafari was a middle school teacher in New York City. "I had no idea how to make clothes," she says.
The post From Teacher to Fashion Brand Founder appeared first on Practical Ecommerce.

title: Costco to flow tariff refunds, ‘if and when’ received, back to customers
description: CEO Ron Vachris said Thursday that the warehouse club retailer is committed to returning any potential refunds to its customers through “lower prices and better values.”

title: The Weekly Closeout: Levi’s closes Dockers sale and Chip Wilson escalates Lululemon battle
description: The jeans maker has officially handed over its khaki label to Authentic Brands Group. Meanwhile, Lululemon’s founder strikes again.

title: Gap revival crystallizes as sales jump 8% in Q4
description: The brand also notched its eighth straight comp rise. Tariffs eroded Gap Inc.’s margins and profits, though, and Athleta sales tanked again.

title: All Eddie Bauer stores to close after failure to find a buyer
description: The entity running the brand’s locations, which licenses the intellectual property from Authentic Brands Group, filed for bankruptcy last month.

title: The dark side of personalization
description: “If you can’t explain it to the customer… then you should not be personalizing,” Qualtrics’ Isabelle Zdatny said.

title: Abercrombie & Fitch sales up 5.4% as online results help growth
description:
Mall apparel staple Abercrombie & Fitch Co. released its Q4 earnings, showing positive numbers with online sales playing a major role. However, the company also flagged possible headwinds. Ultimately, net sales for the retailer increased by 5.4% year over year to $1.67 billion during the quarter. The results marked the 13th consecutive quarter in which […]
The post Abercrombie & Fitch sales up 5.4% as online results help growth appeared first on Digital Commerce 360.

title: OpenAI shifts checkout plans in its agentic commerce strategy
description:
The future of agentic commerce appears to be shifting at OpenAI, where the company’s Instant Checkout feature for ChatGPT is reportedly being sidelined in favor of checkout experiences through merchants that use ChatGPT apps. The Agentic Commerce Protocol (ACP), which OpenAI and the payments vendor Stripe co-developed together, seems poised to live on. However, instead of […]
The post OpenAI shifts checkout plans in its agentic commerce strategy appeared first on Digital Commerce 360.

title: How Costco is addressing tariff changes in early 2026
description:
CEO Ron Vachris opened his address to investors on Costco’s fiscal Q2 earnings call by discussing tariff changes and potential refunds. “The future impact of tariffs remains extremely fluid as the recently eliminated IEEPA tariffs have now been replaced with new global tariffs for at least the next 150 days,” he said. “Our buyers continue […]
The post How Costco is addressing tariff changes in early 2026 appeared first on Digital Commerce 360.

title: DSG expands AI and ecommerce across its distribution brands
description:
Distribution Solutions Group (DSG) is leaning more heavily on ecommerce, artificial intelligence (AI) and digital tools across its business. The industrial and specialty distributor is working to improve customer service, sharpen sales execution and build new growth channels. Executives said on the company’s Q4 earnings call that digital commerce remains a small part of overall […]
The post DSG expands AI and ecommerce across its distribution brands appeared first on Digital Commerce 360.

title: Best Buy online marketplace gains traction as new profit driver
description:
Best Buy’s online marketplace is beginning to emerge as a meaningful contributor to profit, giving the electronics retailer a new source of revenue even as sales in its core business remain uneven. The Minneapolis-based retailer said its U.S. marketplace generated about $300 million in gross merchandise value (GMV) during its fiscal Q4 2026. It now […]
The post Best Buy online marketplace gains traction as new profit driver appeared first on Digital Commerce 360.

title: Is the Pentagon allowed to surveil Americans with AI?
description: The ongoing public feud between the Department of Defense and the AI company Anthropic has raised a deep and still unanswered question: Does the law actually allow the US government to conduct mass surveillance on Americans? Surprisingly, the answer is not straightforward. More than a decade after Edward Snowden exposed the NSA’s collection of bulk…

title: Grammarly is using our identities without permission
description:
Grammarly's "expert review" feature offers to give users writing advice "inspired by" subject matter experts, including recently deceased professors, as Wired reported on Wednesday. When I tried the feature out myself, I found some experts that came as a surprise for a different reason - one of them was my boss. 
The AI-generated feedback included comments that appeared to be from The Verge's editor-in-chief, Nilay Patel, as well as editor-at-large David Pierce and senior editors Sean Hollister and Tom Warren, none of whom gave Grammarly permission to include them in the "expert reviews." 
The feature, which launched in August, claims to h …
Read the full story at The Verge.
